title: arthrophyllum pinnatum
intro: Arthrophyllum pinnatum, also known as the New Zealand mistletoe or wharawhara, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is a small, woody shrub with white flowers and red berries, and is valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The leaves of the Arthrophyllum pinnatum plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat various ailments, and can also be made into a salve or ointment for topical use.
